name: Hybrid Cloud
description: A hybrid cloud architecture spanning both public cloud and on-premises infrastructure. Workloads are distributed based on data sensitivity, latency requirements, and regulatory constraints. Connectivity between environments is established via VPN tunnels or dedicated circuits (e.g., AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute), with unified identity and policy management across both domains.

zones.name: external
zones.description: External actors accessing the hybrid system from the public internet, including end users, partner systems, and SaaS applications that integrate with either the cloud or on-premises components.

zones.name: cloud-dmz
zones.description: The cloud-hosted perimeter layer handling public-facing traffic. Provides DDoS protection, TLS termination, and initial request filtering before traffic reaches cloud application services.

zones.name: cloud-app
zones.description: The cloud application tier hosting workloads suited for elastic scaling, burst capacity, or cloud-native services. Connected to on-premises via secure tunnels for data that must remain on-premises.

zones.name: on-premises
zones.description: The on-premises infrastructure hosting sensitive workloads, legacy systems, and data subject to data residency or sovereignty requirements. Connected to cloud via encrypted tunnel.

zones.name: shared-data
zones.description: The shared data layer accessible from both cloud and on-premises environments. Contains the most sensitive data with strict access controls, encryption, and comprehensive audit logging.

boundaries.name: Cloud Application to Shared Data
controls: Encryption at rest and in transit
Audit logging of all data access

boundaries.name: On-Premises to Shared Data
controls: Encryption at rest and in transit

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph external["External (Untrusted)"]
        user[End User / Browser]
        partner[Partner API]
    end
    subgraph cloud_dmz["Cloud DMZ (Semi-Trusted)"]
        waf[WAF / DDoS]
        clb(Cloud Load Balancer)
        apigw(API Gateway)
    end
    subgraph cloud_app["Cloud Application (Trusted)"]
        csvc1(Cloud Service A)
        csvc2(Cloud Service B)
        cmq([Cloud Message Queue])
    end
    subgraph onprem["On-Premises (Trusted)"]
        opsvc1(Legacy ERP)
        opsvc2(Internal API Server)
        ad(Active Directory)
    end
    subgraph shared["Shared Data (Highly Trusted)"]
        db[(Primary Database)]
        dw[(Data Warehouse)]
        backup[(Encrypted Backup)]
    end
    user -->|HTTPS| waf
    partner -->|HTTPS| waf
    waf --> clb
    clb --> apigw
    apigw -->|OAuth 2.0| csvc1
    apigw -->|OAuth 2.0| csvc2
    csvc1 --> cmq
    cmq --> csvc2
    csvc1 <-->|VPN / mTLS| opsvc1
    csvc2 <-->|VPN / mTLS| opsvc2
    opsvc2 --> ad
    csvc1 -->|Private Endpoint| db
    csvc2 -->|Private Endpoint| dw
    opsvc1 -->|Encrypted| db
    opsvc2 -->|Encrypted| dw
    db -->|Scheduled| backup
